$comment: c145 D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE: a const global with a link-time symbol-address static initializer (const fn-ptr table `const op ctab[]={add10,add20}` + const scalar pointer `int *const gpp=&gbase`) routes to the RelRoConst section kind (relocated-read-only). Each slot carries a load-time relocation; the loader writes the resolved VA, then the call-through / deref reads it -> ctab[0](5)+ctab[1](5)+*gpp = 15+25+2 = 42. In a relocatable object this lands in .data.rel.ro + .rela.data.rel.ro (R_X86_64_64 / R_AARCH64_ABS64) the final linker resolves -- the mechanism that let the real 269k-line sqlite3.c compile to a foreign-linkable sqlite3.o that gcc links + runs. In an exec image it merges into the writable-at-load data segment (ELF .data / PE .rdata base-reloc / Mach-O __DATA dyld-rebase) + relocates in place. A mis-relocated table slot SIGSEGVs on the indirect call; a mis-relocated *gpp shifts the exit off 42. The pe arm RUNS on windows, elf x86_64 on linux, elf arm64 under qemu, macho arm64 on darwin. The byte-level .data.rel.ro/.rela.data.rel.ro emission is red-on-disable in tests/link/test_elf_writer.cpp. The release arm witnesses the optimizer over the const-table load + indirect-call + relro-deref shapes.
